concept: Dye / Coloring Solution / To Color Fabric
description: A substance used to color fabric or other materials; to impart color through soaking. 'Tinoa' names what transforms through saturation—color becoming part of fiber.
sound_symbolism: The word tints deep. 'Ti' touches with initial contact, dye meeting cloth. 'No' soaks inward, color penetrating. Then 'a' opens to complete transformation.
grammatical_notes: Functions as noun: 'lo tinoa' (the dye). As verb: 'mi tinoa' (I dye). For coloring 'wetha' (cloth), 'sima' (thread).

pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Dyeing transformed cloth—indigo, madder, weld, the ancient art of color-fast fabric.
pillars.art-nouveau-aesthetics: Natural hues—plant dyes creating the soft organic colors of the movement.
